title: Notopleura dukei
intro: Notopleura dukei, also known as Duke's notopleura, is a species of plant in the family Rubiaceae, traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties. The health benefits of Notopleura dukei are attributed to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, which have been shown to aid in the treatment of various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of this plant include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Notopleura dukei is commonly used to prepare herbal remedies such as teas, infusions, and extracts, which are traditionally used to treat various health conditions, and is also used in traditional medicine for its antimalarial and antipyretic properties.
taxonomy: Notopleura dukei is classified in the family Rubiaceae. This classification places it in the order Gentianales, which includes other flowering plants with similar characteristics. The classification of Notopleura dukei is based on its morphological and molecular characteristics. Further research may be needed to confirm the exact classification of this species.
